service: VZ Service 1 - Traffic fatalities are preventable incidents.
oneLiner: This work matters because Vision Zero rests on the premise, borrowed from Sweden's program that cut its road death rate in half within a decade, that traffic deaths are the result of fixable street design and policy choices rather than unavoidable accidents.
summary: Mayor Bill de Blasio launched Vision Zero in New York City on January 15, 2014, adapting a road-safety framework Sweden pioneered in 1997. The Swedish approach treats pedestrian and traffic deaths not as inevitable "accidents" but as failures of street design that can be engineered away; Sweden roughly halved its traffic death rate within ten years of adopting it. NYC's program set a goal of eliminating traffic deaths and serious injuries through measures including lower speed limits, redesigned intersections, more enforcement, and expanded use of speed cameras.
